channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan's Army Chief Meets Saudi Defence Minister to Address Iranian Missile and Drone Attacks Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir visited Saudi Arabia and held important talks with Saudi Defence Minister Khalid bin Salman Al Saud on Saturday. The meeting fo...

item.title: Experts Warn Smart Home Devices Are Vulnerable to Hacking Due to Poor Security Practices
item.description: As homes become smarter with Wi-Fi-connected TVs, speakers, thermostats, lights, and voice assistants controlled via apps, they introduce new cybersecurity risks. While such hacks remain uncommon, exp...

item.title: Supreme Court directs judges to protect witnesses and ensure fair cross-examination
item.description: In a major development for Pakistan’s judicial system, the Supreme Court has issued clear directives to protect witnesses from abusive and prolonged cross-examination. Justice Salahuddin Panwhar, writ...

item.title: Veteran Journalist and Former Rajasthan Information Commissioner Narayan Bareth Dies in Jaipur
item.description: Veteran journalist Narayan Bareth, who had a distinguished career with the BBC and later served as Rajasthan Information Commissioner, passed away in Jaipur following a cardiac arrest. He had been und...

item.title: Melbourne Family Purchases Four-Bedroom Home in Wheelers Hill for $1.39 Million
item.description: A four-bedroom home in Wheelers Hill, Melbourne, sold at auction for $1.39 million, exceeding its reserve price by over $100,000. Located at 4 Samantha Close, the clinker brick property is close to Je...

item.title: Lionel Messi and Inter Miami Celebrated at White House by President Trump
item.description: United States President Donald Trump officially welcomed football star Lionel Messi and his club Inter Miami CF to the White House on Thursday, celebrating their victory in the 2025 Major League Socce...

item.title: Manitoba Beef Producers Advocate for Stronger Cattle Safety Measures
item.description: Manitoba Beef Producers are actively lobbying the provincial government to implement stronger safety regulations for cattle. The group is concerned about the increasing risks faced by cattle due to in...

item.title: Monica Juma Takes Top UN Role at Vienna Office Overseeing Drugs and Crime
item.description: Kenya’s National Security Adviser, Monica Kathina Juma, has been appointed to two top leadership positions at the United Nations. UN Secretary-General António Guterres announced that Juma will serve a...

item.title: Lord Mandelson released from bail as investigation into alleged misconduct continues
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson, the former UK ambassador to the United States, has had all bail conditions removed following his arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The Metropolitan Police retur...

item.title: Growing Number of Canadians Seek Evacuation Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Following the escalation of hostilities in the Middle East, tens of thousands of Canadians are now facing significant danger. The conflict intensified after joint U.S.–Israeli strikes on Iran, prompti...

item.title: Five Arrested in Kerala for Smuggling High-End Vehicles from Bhutan, Including a District Transport Officer
item.description: Kochi authorities have arrested five individuals from West Bengal and Assam in connection with the illegal smuggling of high-end used vehicles from Bhutan. According to the Customs Commissionerate, th...

item.title: Live reviews of Melbourne's music and theatre highlights
item.description: Melbourne’s arts scene is alive and thriving, showcased through a series of remarkable live performances. The Hilltop Hoods’ 'Never Coming Home Tour' at Rod Laver Arena was a spectacular display of Au...

item.title: UN agency warns of escalating humanitarian emergency in Middle East
item.description: On Friday, the United Nations refugee agency UNHCR officially described the ongoing conflict in the Middle East as a major humanitarian crisis that urgently needs attention. The situation started last...

item.title: President Tinubu reassures troops and Nigerians of commitment to fight terrorism and maintain peace
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Friday evening make am clear say him government dey determined to fight terrorism and any wahala wey dey threaten peace and unity for Nigeria. The President yan this one...

item.title: Ng’eno’s Mother Appeals to Ruto to Keep Emurua Dikirr MP Seat in Family
item.description: Johana Ng’eno, the MP for Emurua Dikirr in Narok County, passed away in a tragic helicopter crash in late February 2026. Amid the mourning, his mother has come out strongly appealing to President Will...

item.title: Most Indian cities recorded winter air pollution levels above national standards, CREA reports
item.description: During the winter months from October 2025 to February 2026, a study by the Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) revealed that 204 out of 238 Indian cities had PM2.5 levels above the Ind...

item.title: Akani Simbine to host inaugural Simbine Classic in Pretoria with top international athletes
item.description: Sprint star Akani Simbine has announced that he will host the first-ever Simbine Classic in Pretoria on 28 April. With a World Athletics Continental Tour Silver grading, the event will be the highest-...

item.title: Dodoma to Host Regional Women’s Day Celebrations with Over 6,000 Participants
item.description: Preparations are well underway in Dodoma as Tanzania joins the world in celebrating International Women’s Day this Sunday. The regional celebrations will be held at Mbande Primary School grounds in Ko...

item.title: Cup of Joe Launches First International Stardust Tour in Singapore Featuring Yeng Constantino
item.description: The popular Filipino band Cup of Joe is set to begin their Stardust international tour in Singapore this March 8, 2026, at the Arena @ Expo. This concert marks the start of their first-ever tour outsi...

item.title: IGP Disu Remains Professional Amid Sowore’s Criticism, Says Police Spokesman
item.description: The Police Public Relations Officer, ACP Benjamin Hundeyin, don clear ground say the new Inspector General of Police, Tunji Disu, no go allow anybody emotionally manipulate am or make am take unnecess...
